title: Influenza Antiviral Medications: Summary for Clinicians | Influenza (Flu) | CDC
text:

However, clinicians should note that failure to improve or clinical deterioration during oseltamivir or peramivir treatment is more likely to be related to the natural history of acute lung injury and inflammatory damage or onset of other complications (e.g., renal failure, septic shock, ventilator-associated pneumonia) than to emergence of oseltamivir or peramivir resistance. 
Careful attention to ventilator and fluid management and to the prevention and treatment of secondary bacterial pneumonia (e.g., S. pneumoniae , S. pyogenes , and S. aureus , including MRSA) also is critical for severely ill patients ( Bautista, 2010 ; Finelli, 2008 ; Hageman, 2006 ; Harper, 2009; Mandell, 2007; Mauad, 2010 ; Shieh, 2010 ).
